Apple___Apple_scab: Prune and dispose of infected leaves/branches.
Apply appropriate fungicides containing captan or myclobutanil during early spring.
Ensure proper canopy airflow to reduce leaf moisture.
Apple___Black_rot: Remove dead wood and mummified fruits from the tree to reduce spore sources.
Apply protective fungicides like thiophanate-methyl or mancozeb before rain events.
Maintain tree health through proper fertilization and watering routines.
Apple___Cedar_apple_rust: Remove nearby galls from cedar/juniper trees to break the rust lifecycle.
Apply preventive fungicidal sprays containing myclobutanil before petal fall.
Plant resistant fruit tree cultivars when establishing new orchards.
Apple___healthy: Ensure sufficient watering and optimal drainage.
Maintain standard preventive fungicide and pruning schedules.
Cherry_(including_sour)___Powdery_mildew: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate-based fungicidal sprays early in the season.
Avoid overhead irrigation to keep foliage reasonably dry.
Prune inner branches to improve sunlight penetration.
Cherry_(including_sour)___healthy: Monitor water stress and soil nutrition regularly.
Conduct preventive dormant spraying to manage overwintering pests.
Maintain a clean orchard floor free of fallen debris.
Corn_(maize)___Cercospora_leaf_spot Gray: Implement crop rotation techniques, avoiding planting corn on corn.
Apply foliar fungicides such as azoxystrobin or pyraclostrobin at the VT to R1 growth stages.
Use resistant corn hybrids to minimize long term yield loss.
Corn_(maize)___Common_rust_: Scout fields heavily right before tasseling.
Apply fungicide treatments if rust spots are observed on multiple lower leaves before flowering.
Consider planting genetically resistant maize hybrids in subsequent seasons.
Corn_(maize)___Northern_Leaf_Blight: Tillage practices can help bury infected crop residue to reduce initial inoculum.
Treat carefully with foliar fungicides like strobilurin/triazole premixes when conditions are highly humid.
Rotate fields to non-host crops like soybeans or alfalfa.
Corn_(maize)___healthy: Provide timely nitrogen and phosphorus fertilization.
Monitor irrigation carefully around silking and tasseling phases.
Conduct regular field scouting to maintain disease-free status.
Grape___Black_rot: Destroy any mummified fruit left on the vines from the previous season.
Apply protectant fungicides (mancozeb, captan) aggressively starting at early shoot growth.
Promote good air circulation through careful canopy management.
Grape___Esca_(Black_Measles): Adopt preventative pruning measures and avoid executing large unsealed pruning cuts.
Prune infected vines later in the season during dry periods to avoid spore spread.
Remove completely dead or dying cordons promptly and burn them.
Grape___Leaf_blight_(Isariopsis_Leaf_Spo: Improve vineyard drainage and canopy aeration.
Apply copper-based fungicides or broad-spectrum controls right after bloom.
Clear the previous season's leaf debris from under the trellis.
Grape___healthy: Maintain balanced crop loads using appropriate thinning.
Control canopy density through summer pruning and leaf removal if necessary.
Administer regular preventative nutrient sprays based on soil assays.
Peach___Bacterial_spot: Utilize copper-based bactericides during late dormancy and early spring.
Maintain a strict balanced nutrient profile, as over-fertilized trees become highly susceptible.
Avoid abrasive sandblasting near orchards to prevent creating infection pathways on leaves.
Peach___healthy: Continue regular dormant oil application to deter scale and mites.
Maintain summer pruning to encourage light exposure and airflow.
Keep the orchard floor clear of fallen mummified fruit.
Pepper,_bell___Bacterial_spot: Avoid overhead watering by switching to drip irrigation to keep pepper leaves dry.
Routinely spray fixed copper mixed with mancozeb as a protectant protocol.
Practice strict a minimum 3-year crop rotation out of solanaceous crops.
Pepper,_bell___healthy: Provide consistent soil moisture to prevent blossom end rot.
Perform routine scouting for aphids and thrips to maintain health.
Maintain proper fertilization specifically focusing on calcium and potassium.
Potato___Early_blight: Use targeted early-blight fungicides like chlorothalonil upon canopy closure.
Keep potato plants adequately watered to prevent drought stress, avoiding the extreme dry/wet cycles.
Harvest tubers carefully only after the vines are completely dead to prevent skin damage.
Potato___Late_blight: Apply protective fungicides preemptively, especially when the weather is consistently moist and cool.
Destroy any infected volunteer potatoes and cull piles entirely prior to new planting.
Harvest during dry weather components and ensure tubers are dry before storing.
Potato___healthy: Continue uniform drip or sprinkler irrigation.
Hasten timely hilling techniques to protect emerging tubers from sun and pests.
Apply preventive seed-piece treatments before planting.
Strawberry___Leaf_scorch: Eliminate infected foliage, heavily infected plants, and nearby weed reservoirs.
Apply broad-spectrum fungicides such as captan or fixed copper early in the spring season.
Ensure wide plant spacing allowing crowns sufficient air circulation and rapid drying.
Strawberry___healthy: Replace old plants completely every few years as yields inevitably decrease.
Utilize clean organic straw mulch to keep berries elevated off damp soil.
Ensure consistent drip watering keeping foliage perfectly dry during fruiting.
